ddls:
CREATE TABLE "areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the area */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the area */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the area */,
  "area_size_sq_km" REAL /* Size of the area in square kilometers */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the area */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the area */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of area (urban, rural, metropolitan) */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "sector_code" TEXT /* Standardized sector code (e.g., NAICS or SIC) */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "business_counts" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the area */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sector */,
  "num_businesses" INTEGER /* Number of businesses in the sector for the area */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "quarter" TEXT /* Quarter of the data (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id", "sector_id", "year", "quarter")
)

CREATE TABLE "businesses" (
  "business_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each business */,
  "business_name" TEXT /* Name of the business */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the area where the business is located */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sector of the business */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the business */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the business */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the business */,
  "business_type" TEXT /* Type of business (sole proprietorship, partnership, corporation, non-profit) */,
  "employee_count" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the business */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date the business was established */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("business_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_businesses_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_businesses_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "business_financials" (
  "business_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the business */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the financial data */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the business */,
  "expenses" REAL /* Annual expenses of the business */,
  "profit" REAL /* Annual profit of the business */,
  "asset_value" REAL /* Total asset value of the business */,
  "liability_value" REAL /* Total liability value of the business */,
  "cash_flow" REAL /* Annual cash flow of the business */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("business_id", "year")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the area */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the economic data */,
  "gdp" REAL /* Gross Domestic Product (GDP) of the area */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate in the area */,
  "inflation_rate" REAL /* Inflation rate in the area */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Poverty rate in the area */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median income in the area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id", "year")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, planner, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_table" TEXT /* Table name of the data accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data entry */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the area */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sector */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the historical data */,
  "num_businesses" INTEGER /* Number of businesses in the sector for the area */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue for the sector in the area */,
  "expenses" REAL /* Total expenses for the sector in the area */,
  "profit" REAL /* Total profit for the sector in the area */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the historical data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality or reliability of the historical data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "external_data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each external data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the external data source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data provided by the source */,
  "data_url" TEXT /* URL to access the data */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the data was last updated */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the external data (e.g., CSV, JSON, XML) */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of updates to the external data (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
